title: poa leptocoma subsp. paucispicula
intro: Poa leptocoma subsp. paucispicula, also known as the mountain bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The mountain bluegrass has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Poa leptocoma subsp. paucispicula typically involve infusions or decoctions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical applications.
